title: Add M8 UI, responsive layout, and zh-CN/en localization gates
summary: Amend the reviewed future M8 entry prompt and create a reviewed M8 task DAG so Owner UI, responsive layout and zh-CN/en localization gates become mandatory M8 productization work before implementation starts.
acceptance: Records startup audit evidence confirming M0-M7 are done, WM-0110 is done/verified/integrated/pushed, main and origin/main are synchronized, inbox is 0, worktree was clean before this task, only the main worktree was present and M8 was not already started

Audits the reviewed future M8 entry prompt against OWNER-AMENDMENT-2026-06-27-UI-I18N-PRODUCTIZATION and records whether Product UI, Responsive Layout, Localization, Visual Identity, First Playability and Accessibility gates were missing or insufficient

Amends the M8 entry prompt or records a reviewed addendum so UI productization, required viewport coverage, zh-CN/en localization, language selection, default player HUD versus debug overlay separation, first-play guidance and accessibility are mandatory M8 gates

Creates a reviewed M8 task DAG with concrete task packets covering M8 planning/scope verification, UI productization design system, responsive layout and screenshot tests, localization infrastructure and string extraction, system language detection and manual override, main menu/settings/language selection, default player HUD versus debug overlay separation, first-play guidance, accessibility/readability, content/localization completeness, M8 content/1.0 closure work, validation matrix, closeout and future handoff
Keeps downstream M8 packets proposed until WM-0111 is independently reviewed, integrated and marked done; no M8 implementation task is claimed before that

Does not implement product UI/runtime behavior, create public release artifacts, submit stores, sign builds, enable telemetry/accounts/paid services, make final privacy/legal/store/save claims or start post-M8 work

history.details: Created after Owner started M8 and supplied OWNER-AMENDMENT-2026-06-27-UI-I18N-PRODUCTIZATION; WM-0110 is done and M8 has not started.
WM-0110 is done, verified, integrated and pushed; WM-0111 is the M8 scope amendment/planning gate required before implementation.
M8 UI/i18n/responsive scope amendment and proposed M8 task DAG drafted; downstream M8 tasks remain proposed pending independent review and integration.

verified: No blocking findings. Owner UI/i18n/responsive amendment is incorporated as mandatory M8 gates; current Roadmap authority and owner-gated release boundaries are preserved; downstream WM-0112..WM-0127 packets are proposed, dependency-valid and acyclic; validation evidence confirmed.

Independent reviewer verified WM-0111; post-review integration gate passed including handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm quality, pnpm ci:local, m5-invariants, bench isolated rerun and 100k M5 sim.

objective: Make the Owner UI/i18n/responsive amendment a reviewed M8 product gate and create the M8 task DAG without starting implementation.
forbiddenPaths: telemetry account paid service or crash upload implementation
final privacy legal or store claims
post-M8 task creation, promotion, claim, implementation or review
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: M8 UI/i18n amendment could be treated as optional polish; task must make it a required M8 closeout gate.
Risk: planning could accidentally start UI implementation; file ownership forbids apps/packages/tools changes in WM-0111.
Risk: task DAG could overclaim 1.0 release readiness; owner-gated public release, store, signing, telemetry, accounts, paid services and final claims must remain blocked.
File ownership is limited to M8 prompt/amendment reports, M8 task packets and narrow roadmap/project-state markers.
ownerGate: Block only if Owner amendment conflicts with locked decisions; otherwise incorporate it through reviewed M8 planning.

Do not execute public release, 1.0 release, Early Access launch, store submission, Web public launch, signed Windows installer, telemetry, account system, paid service, crash upload or final privacy/legal/store/save-compatibility commitments.
Do not change Web verdict away from demo-only or Windows verdict beyond controlled external test without owner approval.
Do not implement M8 UI/runtime work until WM-0111 is independently verified, integrated and done.
Preserve M0-M7 regression gates and benchmark thresholds.
m0M7RegressionCoverage: Preserve all closed task states and review history through WM-0110.
Preserve M5 final world/read-model hashes 0xfba70a5c / 0x9ba83cb7 unless a reviewed migration changes them.
Preserve M6 Web demo-only verdict and Windows unsigned controlled-external-test verdict.
Preserve M7 EA/public playtest preparation artifacts and owner-gated decisions.
Keep Simulation Worker or Node headless as the only authoritative world writer.
benchmarkImpact: No product benchmark baseline or artifact changes. WM-0111 records M8 benchmark and UI/i18n validation requirements only and preserves the 10 percent warning / 20 percent blocking thresholds.
reviewRouting: Project-director completes to reviewer. Reviewer must verify that Owner UI/i18n/responsive gates are mandatory in the M8 DAG, downstream packets are proposed/unclaimed, no implementation started and owner gates are preserved.
rollbackModel: Revert WM-0111 task/report files and newly created downstream M8 task JSON packets; leave WM-0110 verified closeout and future M8 prompt intact and M8 implementation unstarted.

sparkEligibility.reason: M8 scope amendment, product gate design, task DAG design, owner gates and final review cannot be assigned to Spark.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Owner UI/i18n/responsive amendment is incorporated as mandatory M8 gates; current Roadmap authority and owner-gated release boundaries are preserved; downstream WM-0112..WM-0127 packets are proposed, dependency-valid and acyclic; validation evidence confirmed.

integration.summary: Independent reviewer verified WM-0111; post-review integration gate passed including handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm quality, pnpm ci:local, m5-invariants, bench isolated rerun and 100k M5 sim.
